content:

title: Tech layoffs 2026: Meta plans to 8,000 job cuts; over 73,000 roles lost globally in 3 months
description: Layoffs across the global tech industry are accelerating in 2026, with more than 73,000 jobs already cut in just three months. From Meta to Oracle, companies are trimming workforces while doubling down on artificial intelligence, signalling a deeper shift in how tech giants are restructuring for the future.
content:
The wave of tech layoffs in 2026 is showing no signs of slowing down. Job cuts are sweeping across the global technology sector in 2026, as companies move from cautious belt-tightening to more aggressive restructuring.

title: AI-first filmmaking model debuts with Jio Studios' 'Krishna'
description: Jio Studios and Collective Artists Network have revealed the teaser for their new film Krishna. This movie uses an AI-native filmmaking process. The technology, called Galleri5, integrates artificial intelligence throughout production. This aims to improve efficiency and lower costs. It also makes advanced tools more accessible.

title: Blind runner completes Brighton Marathon using AI glasses
description: A blind artist achieved a world first by running a marathon guided by artificial intelligence glasses, assisted by remote volunteers.
content:
Clarke Reynolds, 45, from Portsmouth, finished the Brighton Marathon on Sunday, using Meta AI glasses paired with the Be My Eyes app, which enlists volunteers across the world to support visually impaired people in daily tasks.
Mr Reynolds, who has j... [1642 chars]

title: India brings AI services to villages with unique DPI: Report
description: India is pairing artificial intelligence with digital public infrastructure, local‑language systems and governance safeguards, helping farmers, women and village institutions can use AI directly rather than through urban intermediaries, a report has said.

title: South Korea's Lee to seek big boost in economic ties in summit with PM Modi
description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung is in New Delhi for talks with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The visit aims to significantly increase economic ties, focusing on shipbuilding. Both nations plan to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030. Cooperation may also expand into finance, artificial intelligence, and defense. India's role in global production is highlighted.

title: Mouser Electronics Explores How Artificial Intelligence Shapes Everyday Technologies and Experiences
description: **media[1019732]**SHANGHAI, April 20, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Mouser Electronics, Inc., the authorized global distributor with the newest electronic components and industrial automation products, today announced the first 2026 installment of its Empowering Innovation Together (EIT) technology series, Engineering AI for Daily Life. This installment explores how artificial intelligence is increasingly embedded in everyday products and services, from assisted search and messaging tools to healthcare wearables that monitor personal well-being. As AI capabilities expand across consumer and connected devices, engineers continue to design systems that make these technologies more useful, intuitive, and trustworthy in real-world applications.**media[1019733]**

title: Harsh Goenka shares Mumbai’s new obsession after Labubu, mahjong and padel, and you may have tried it too
description: Industrialist Harsh Goenka has creatively embraced artificial intelligence to illustrate Mumbai's burgeoning benne dosa craze. His first AI-generated image, a charming vintage-style poster, humorously depicts long queues and the irresistible allure of butter-drenched dosas and filter coffee ice cream, resonating with local food enthusiasts.

title: Siemens Threatens to Skip Europe for AI Spending Due to Rules
description: Siemens AG will prioritize investments in the US and China if the European Union doesn’t change its artificial intelligence regulations, Chief Executive Officer Roland Busch said.
content:
Siemens AG will prioritize investments in the US and China if the European Union doesn’t change its artificial intelligence regulations, Chief Executive Officer Roland Busch said.

title: US security agency is using Anthropic’s Mythos despite blacklist: Report

content:
